paper_title: Inferred from text: Paleoclimate reconstruction using pollen data from Lago Chungará

methods.description: Process for collecting sediment cores from Lago Chungará and surface pollen samples from the western Andes slope transect for palynological analysis.

steps.description: In November 2002, 15 sediment cores up to 8 m long were obtained from different parts of the lake using a raft equipped with a Kullenberg corer (Sáez et al., 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment cores from a lake using a Kullenberg corer.

steps.description: The fossil pollen sequence presented here was developed from core 7, which was obtained from the northwestern border of the lake at 18 m of water depth (Sáez et al., 2007;Fig. 1b).
steps.searchable_summary: Select a specific sediment core (core 7) for fossil pollen analysis.

steps.description: In addition, we obtained pollen data from 26 surface samples collected along a west-east transect on the western Andes slope at 18 • S with the aim of determining the pollen signals associated with modern vegetation belts.Surface pollen samples were obtained from exposed soils located at regular intervals of ∼ 100 m, from 2100 to 4400 m a.s.l.Thus, a surface pollen transect was formed crossing all vegetation belts established across the western Andean slopes up to the base of Lago Chungará (Fig. 1a).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect surface pollen samples along an altitudinal transect on a mountain slope.

methods.description: Process for obtaining climate data corresponding to the surface pollen sample locations to document associated climate gradients.

steps.description: Furthermore, we obtained annual temperature and summer precipitation data for each surface sample point from the WorldClim2 dataset (Fick and Hijmans, 2017), with the aim of documenting the climate gradients associated with the pollen transect.
steps.searchable_summary: Download climate data (temperature, precipitation) from WorldClim2 for specific locations.
inputs: Surface sample locations (coordinates inferred from altitude/transect description)

methods.description: Standard laboratory procedures for preparing both fossil and surface pollen samples for microscopic analysis.

steps.description: The 26 surface samples plus 49 fossil pollen samples from core 7 were prepared following standard procedures for palynological analysis (Faegri and Iversen, 1989) in the Laboratory of Paleoecology and Paleoclimatology of CEAZA, La Serena, Chile.
steps.searchable_summary: Process fossil pollen samples using standard palynological laboratory techniques.
outputs: Prepared fossil pollen slides/samples ready for counting

steps.description: The 26 surface samples plus 49 fossil pollen samples from core 7 were prepared following standard procedures for palynological analysis (Faegri and Iversen, 1989) in the Laboratory of Paleoecology and Paleoclimatology of CEAZA, La Serena, Chile.
steps.searchable_summary: Process surface pollen samples using standard palynological laboratory techniques.
outputs: Prepared surface pollen slides/samples ready for counting

methods.description: Procedures for calculating relative pollen percentages and pollen accumulation rates from raw pollen counts.

steps.description: Pollen data are expressed as a relative percentage, which was calculated from the sum of a minimum of 300 terrestrial pollen grains.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate relative percentage of terrestrial pollen based on a minimum count sum.
outputs: Relative percentages of terrestrial pollen taxa

steps.description: The percentage of aquatic pollen was calculated from a sum that included all terrestrial and aquatic pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate relative percentage of aquatic pollen based on the sum of all pollen grains.
inputs: Raw pollen counts (terrestrial and aquatic taxa)
outputs: Relative percentages of aquatic pollen taxa

steps.description: Pollen accumulation rate (PAR; particles yr −1 cm −2 ) was calculated for terrestrial and aquatic pollen using data from the age-depth model published by Giralt et al. (2008).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Pollen Accumulation Rate (PAR) using pollen counts and an age-depth model.
outputs: Pollen Accumulation Rate (PAR) for terrestrial pollen
Pollen Accumulation Rate (PAR) for aquatic pollen

methods.description: Statistical procedures applied to the calculated pollen data to identify major changes in the pollen sequence.

steps.description: The statistical analysis of pollen data included a stratigraphically constrained cluster analysis (CONISS) for the identification of major pollen changes carried out with the software Tilia (Grimm, 1987).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform stratigraphically constrained cluster analysis (CONISS) on pollen data.
